{"type":"assistant","message":{"model":"claude-sonnet-4-6","id":"msg_01SPGDmAS3P2N6ZrRXFwCDcL","type":"message","role":"assistant","content":[{"type":"tool_use","id":"toolu_01UYEtYZu7HKk6Frba1rFRZq","name":"Read","input":{"file_path":"/root/Projects/bookmate/app/app/api/telegram/webhook/route.ts"},"caller":{"type":"direct"}}],"stop_reason":null,"stop_sequence":null,"usage":{"input_tokens":1,"cache_creation_input_tokens":7739,"cache_read_input_tokens":39951,"cache_creation":{"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":7739,"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":0},"output_tokens":34,"service_tier":"standard","inference_geo":"not_available"},"context_management":null},"parent_tool_use_id":"toolu_01VRb8XuH96FMHgWe6LjJa4W","session_id":"157c1bc9-ab32-4587-b8cf-8c8afacd8f33","uuid":"06a1c429-eb1b-4aea-96af-77ccdd8cbad8"}
{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_01UYEtYZu7HKk6Frba1rFRZq","type":"tool_result","content":"     1→import { NextRequest, NextResponse } from \"next/server\";\n     2→import db from \"@/lib/db\";\n     3→import {\n     4→  createChatInviteLink,\n     5→  exportChatInviteLink,\n     6→  sendMessage,\n     7→  parseListingIdFromPayload,\n     8→  escapeHtml,\n     9→} from \"@/lib/telegram\";\n    10→import { createNotification } from \"@/lib/notifications\";\n    11→import { safeCompare } from \"@/lib/crypto-utils\";\n    12→\n    13→/**\n    14→ * Telegram webhook endpoint.\n    15→ *\n    16→ * Handles two types of updates:\n    17→ *\n    18→ * 1. `my_chat_member` — bot was added to a group\n    19→ *    When the author creates a group via the deep link, the bot is\n    20→ *    automatically added. We detect this, generate an invite link,\n    21→ *    and save it to the listing.\n    22→ *\n    23→ * 2. `message` with `/start listing_ID` — the bot receives a start\n    24→ *    command in a group with a payload referencing a listing.\n    25→ */\n    26→export async function POST(req: NextRequest) {\n    27→  // Verify webhook secret — fail closed if not configured\n    28→  const webhookSecret = process.env.TELEGRAM_WEBHOOK_SECRET;\n    29→  if (!webhookSecret) {\n    30→    return NextResponse.json({ error: \"Webhook not configured\" }, { status: 500 });\n    31→  }\n    32→  const headerSecret = req.headers.get(\"X-Telegram-Bot-Api-Secret-Token\");\n    33→  if (!headerSecret || !safeCompare(headerSecret, webhookSecret)) {\n    34→    return NextResponse.json({ error: \"Unauthorized\" }, { status: 403 });\n    35→  }\n    36→\n    37→  let update: Record<string, unknown>;\n    38→  try {\n    39→    update = await req.json();\n    40→  } catch {\n    41→    return NextResponse.json({ error: \"Invalid JSON\" }, { status: 400 });\n    42→  }\n    43→\n    44→  // Handle bot being added to a group chat\n    45→  if (update.my_chat_member) {\n    46→    await handleChatMemberUpdate(\n    47→      update.my_chat_member as ChatMemberUpdate\n    48→    );\n    49→  }\n    50→\n    51→  // Handle /start command in groups (fallback mechanism)\n    52→  if (update.message) {\n    53→    await handleMessage(update.message as TelegramMessage);\n    54→  }\n    55→\n    56→  // Always return 200 to Telegram to avoid retries\n    57→  return NextResponse.json({ ok: true });\n    58→}\n    59→\n    60→interface ChatMemberUpdate {\n    61→  chat: {\n    62→    id: number;\n    63→    title?: string;\n    64→    type: string;\n    65→  };\n    66→  from: {\n    67→    id: number;\n    68→    first_name: string;\n    69→  };\n    70→  new_chat_member: {\n    71→    user: {\n    72→      id: number;\n    73→      is_bot: boolean;\n    74→    };\n    75→    status: string;\n    76→  };\n    77→}\n    78→\n    79→interface TelegramMessage {\n    80→  chat: {\n    81→    id: number;\n    82→    type: string;\n    83→    title?: string;\n    84→  };\n    85→  text?: string;\n    86→  from?: {\n    87→    id: number;\n    88→  };\n    89→}\n    90→\n    91→/**\n    92→ * When the bot is added to a group, check if it's related to a listing.\n    93→ *\n    94→ * The deep link format is: t.me/BOT?startgroup=listing_ID\n    95→ * When a user creates a group through this link, Telegram:\n    96→ * 1. Creates the group\n    97→ * 2. Adds the bot as a member\n    98→ * 3. Sends a `my_chat_member` update\n    99→ *\n   100→ * We use the group title or a pending mapping to associate the chat with a listing.\n   101→ */\n   102→async function handleChatMemberUpdate(update: ChatMemberUpdate) {\n   103→  const { chat, new_chat_member } = update;\n   104→\n   105→  // Only process when bot is added (member or administrator)\n   106→  if (\n   107→    !new_chat_member.user.is_bot ||\n   108→    ![\"member\", \"administrator\"].includes(new_chat_member.status)\n   109→  ) {\n   110→    return;\n   111→  }\n   112→\n   113→  // Only process group chats\n   114→  if (![\"group\", \"supergroup\"].includes(chat.type)) {\n   115→    return;\n   116→  }\n   117→\n   118→  // Try to find the listing this group belongs to.\n   119→  // Strategy 1: Check pending_telegram_groups mapping table\n   120→  const pending = db\n   121→    .prepare(\n   122→      \"SELECT listing_id FROM pending_telegram_groups WHERE telegram_chat_id = ?\"\n   123→    )\n   124→    .get(chat.id) as { listing_id: number } | undefined;\n   125→\n   126→  if (pending) {\n   127→    await linkTelegramToListing(pending.listing_id, chat.id);\n   128→    db.prepare(\"DELETE FROM pending_telegram_groups WHERE telegram_chat_id = ?\").run(\n   129→      chat.id\n   130→    );\n   131→    return;\n   132→  }\n   133→\n   134→  // Strategy 2: Check for listings awaiting auto-telegram (most recent unlinked full listing by this author)\n   135→  // We store the chat ID and wait for the /start command or title match\n   136→  // For now, store the mapping and send a welcome message\n   137→  await sendMessage(\n   138→    chat.id,\n   139→    \"Hello! I'm the Bookmate bot. I'll help set up this reading group.\\n\\n\" +\n   140→      'If this group was created for a Bookmate listing, I\\'ll automatically generate an invite link. ' +\n   141→      \"The organizer can also type <code>/link</code> to manually trigger the setup.\"\n   142→  );\n   143→\n   144→  // Store the chat for potential later linking\n   145→  try {\n   146→    db.prepare(\n   147→      \"INSERT OR IGNORE INTO telegram_chats (chat_id, chat_title, created_at) VALUES (?, ?, datetime('now'))\"\n   148→    ).run(chat.id, chat.title || \"\");\n   149→  } catch {\n   150→    // Table might not exist yet on first run — handled gracefully\n   151→  }\n   152→}\n   153→\n   154→/**\n   155→ * Handle messages in group chats.\n   156→ * Supports /start with payload and /link command.\n   157→ */\n   158→async function handleMessage(message: TelegramMessage) {\n   159→  if (!message.text || ![\"group\", \"supergroup\"].includes(message.chat.type)) {\n   160→    return;\n   161→  }\n   162→\n   163→  const text = message.text.trim();\n   164→\n   165→  // Handle /start with listing payload (sent when group is created via deep link)\n   166→  if (text.startsWith(\"/start \")) {\n   167→    const payload = text.replace(\"/start \", \"\").trim();\n   168→    const listingId = parseListingIdFromPayload(payload);\n   169→    if (listingId) {\n   170→      // Verify the sender is the listing author to prevent unauthorized linking\n   171→      const telegramUserId = message.from?.id;\n   172→      if (!telegramUserId) {\n   173→        await sendMessage(message.chat.id, \"Could not identify the sender.\");\n   174→        return;\n   175→      }\n   176→\n   177→      const linkedUser = db\n   178→        .prepare(\"SELECT user_id FROM telegram_user_links WHERE telegram_user_id = ?\")\n   179→        .get(telegramUserId) as { user_id: number } | undefined;\n   180→\n   181→      if (linkedUser) {\n   182→        const listing = db\n   183→          .prepare(\"SELECT author_id FROM listings WHERE id = ?\")\n   184→          .get(listingId) as { author_id: number } | undefined;\n   185→\n   186→        if (listing && listing.author_id === linkedUser.user_id) {\n   187→          await linkTelegramToListing(listingId, message.chat.id);\n   188→          return;\n   189→        }\n   190→      }\n   191→\n   192→      // If no linked user or not the author, store for later linking via /link command\n   193→      await sendMessage(\n   194→        message.chat.id,\n   195→        \"To link this group to a Bookmate listing, the listing author should use the <code>/link</code> command.\"\n   196→      );\n   197→      return;\n   198→    }\n   199→  }\n   200→\n   201→  // Handle /link command — allow manual linking\n   202→  // Security: only show listings authored by a Bookmate user linked to this Telegram user\n   203→  if (text === \"/link\") {\n   204→    const telegramUserId = message.from?.id;\n   205→    if (!telegramUserId) {\n   206→      await sendMessage(message.chat.id, \"Could not identify the sender.\");\n   207→      return;\n   208→    }\n   209→\n   210→    // Look up the Bookmate user linked to this Telegram user\n   211→    const linkedUser = db\n   212→      .prepare(\"SELECT user_id FROM telegram_user_links WHERE telegram_user_id = ?\")\n   213→      .get(telegramUserId) as { user_id: number } | undefined;\n   214→\n   215→    if (!linkedUser) {\n   216→      await sendMessage(\n   217→        message.chat.id,\n   218→        \"Your Telegram account is not linked to a Bookmate account. \" +\n   219→          \"Please use the Telegram setup link from your Bookmate listing page to link your account first.\"\n   220→      );\n   221→      return;\n   222→    }\n   223→\n   224→    // Only show unlinked listings authored by this verified user\n   225→    const unlinkedListings = db\n   226→      .prepare(\n   227→        `SELECT id, book_title FROM listings\n   228→         WHERE is_full = 1 AND (telegram_link = '' OR telegram_link IS NULL)\n   229→         AND telegram_chat_id IS NULL\n   230→         AND author_id = ?\n   231→         ORDER BY created_at DESC LIMIT 5`\n   232→      )\n   233→      .all(linkedUser.user_id) as { id: number; book_title: string }[];\n   234→\n   235→    if (unlinkedListings.length === 0) {\n   236→      await sendMessage(\n   237→        message.chat.id,\n   238→        \"No unlinked reading groups found for your account. The group may already be set up!\"\n   239→      );\n   240→      return;\n   241→    }\n   242→\n   243→    if (unlinkedListings.length === 1) {\n   244→      await linkTelegramToListing(unlinkedListings[0].id, message.chat.id);\n   245→      return;\n   246→    }\n   247→\n   248→    const listText = unlinkedListings\n   249→      .map((l) => `  /link_${l.id} — \"${escapeHtml(l.book_title)}\"`)\n   250→      .join(\"\\n\");\n   251→    await sendMessage(\n   252→      message.chat.id,\n   253→      `Multiple reading groups need a Telegram link. Pick one:\\n\\n${listText}`\n   254→    );\n   255→    return;\n   256→  }\n   257→\n   258→  // Handle /link_ID command — require verified ownership\n   259→  const linkMatch = text.match(/^\\/link_(\\d+)$/);\n   260→  if (linkMatch) {\n   261→    const telegramUserId = message.from?.id;\n   262→    if (!telegramUserId) {\n   263→      await sendMessage(message.chat.id, \"Could not identify the sender.\");\n   264→      return;\n   265→    }\n   266→\n   267→    const linkedUser = db\n   268→      .prepare(\"SELECT user_id FROM telegram_user_links WHERE telegram_user_id = ?\")\n   269→      .get(telegramUserId) as { user_id: number } | undefined;\n   270→\n   271→    if (!linkedUser) {\n   272→      await sendMessage(\n   273→        message.chat.id,\n   274→        \"Your Telegram account is not linked to a Bookmate account.\"\n   275→      );\n   276→      return;\n   277→    }\n   278→\n   279→    const listingId = parseInt(linkMatch[1], 10);\n   280→\n   281→    // Verify this user is the listing author and the listing is full\n   282→    const listing = db\n   283→      .prepare(\"SELECT author_id, is_full FROM listings WHERE id = ?\")\n   284→      .get(listingId) as { author_id: number; is_full: number } | undefined;\n   285→\n   286→    if (!listing || listing.author_id !== linkedUser.user_id) {\n   287→      await sendMessage(\n   288→        message.chat.id,\n   289→        \"You can only link Telegram groups to your own Bookmate listings.\"\n   290→      );\n   291→      return;\n   292→    }\n   293→\n   294→    if (!listing.is_full) {\n   295→      await sendMessage(\n   296→        message.chat.id,\n   297→        \"This listing's group is not full yet. You can link a Telegram group once the group reaches its maximum size.\"\n   298→      );\n   299→      return;\n   300→    }\n   301→\n   302→    await linkTelegramToListing(listingId, message.chat.id);\n   303→  }\n   304→}\n   305→\n   306→/**\n   307→ * Link a Telegram chat to a listing:\n   308→ * 1. Generate an invite link\n   309→ * 2. Save it to the listing\n   310→ * 3. Notify all members\n   311→ * 4. Send a confirmation message to the Telegram group\n   312→ */\n   313→async function linkTelegramToListing(listingId: number, chatId: number) {\n   314→  const listing = db\n   315→    .prepare(\"SELECT * FROM listings WHERE id = ?\")\n   316→    .get(listingId) as Record<string, unknown> | undefined;\n   317→\n   318→  if (!listing) {\n   319→    await sendMessage(chatId, \"Could not find that reading group on Bookmate.\");\n   320→    return;\n   321→  }\n   322→\n   323→  if (listing.telegram_link) {\n   324→    await sendMessage(chatId, \"This reading group already has a Telegram link set up!\");\n   325→    return;\n   326→  }\n   327→\n   328→  // Try to create an invite link\n   329→  let inviteLink = await createChatInviteLink(chatId);\n   330→  if (!inviteLink) {\n   331→    // Fallback: export the default invite link\n   332→    inviteLink = await exportChatInviteLink(chatId);\n   333→  }\n   334→\n   335→  if (!inviteLink) {\n   336→    await sendMessage(\n   337→      chatId,\n   338→      \"I couldn't generate an invite link. Please make sure I have admin permissions in this group, \" +\n   339→        \"then try again with /link\"\n   340→    );\n   341→    return;\n   342→  }\n   343→\n   344→  // Save to database\n   345→  db.prepare(\n   346→    \"UPDATE listings SET telegram_link = ?, telegram_chat_id = ? WHERE id = ?\"\n   347→  ).run(inviteLink, chatId, listingId);\n   348→\n   349→  // Notify all members on Bookmate\n   350→  const members = db\n   351→    .prepare(\"SELECT user_id FROM listing_members WHERE listing_id = ?\")\n   352→    .all(listingId) as { user_id: number }[];\n   353→\n   354→  const bookTitle = listing.book_title as string;\n   355→  for (const member of members) {\n   356→    createNotification(\n   357→      member.user_id,\n   358→      listingId,\n   359→      \"telegram_ready\",\n   360→      `The Telegram group for \"${bookTitle}\" is ready! Click to view the invite link.`\n   361→    );\n   362→  }\n   363→\n   364→  // Send confirmation to the Telegram group\n   365→  // Escape book title to prevent HTML injection via Telegram's HTML parser\n   366→  await sendMessage(\n   367→    chatId,\n   368→    `This group is now linked to the Bookmate reading group for \"<b>${escapeHtml(bookTitle)}</b>\".\\n\\n` +\n   369→      `Invite link: ${inviteLink}\\n\\n` +\n   370→      \"All group members have been notified on Bookmate. Happy reading!\"\n   371→  );\n   372→}\n   373→"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":"toolu_01VRb8XuH96FMHgWe6LjJa4W","session_id":"157c1bc9-ab32-4587-b8cf-8c8afacd8f33","uuid":"6c066754-afde-40a4-8de5-85fbf544aeb5","timestamp":"2026-03-24T10:01:55.052Z"}
{"type":"system","subtype":"task_progress","task_id":"abacbd2cccabbeabe","tool_use_id":"toolu_01VRb8XuH96FMHgWe6LjJa4W","description":"Reading app/api/discord/webhook/route.ts","usage":{"total_tokens":48678,"tool_uses":29,"duration_ms":44106},"last_tool_name":"Read","uuid":"6344d4cb-3c86-4dde-9fcd-60fcefb6b1c3","session_id":"157c1bc9-ab32-4587-b8cf-8c8afacd8f33"}
{"type":"assistant","message":{"model":"claude-sonnet-4-6","id":"msg_01SPGDmAS3P2N6ZrRXFwCDcL","type":"message","role":"assistant","content":[{"type":"tool_use","id":"toolu_01TXxCmWqnbTTxWcBhCFraYf","name":"Read","input":{"file_path":"/root/Projects/bookmate/app/app/api/discord/webhook/route.ts"},"caller":{"type":"direct"}}],"stop_reason":null,"stop_sequence":null,"usage":{"input_tokens":1,"cache_creation_input_tokens":7739,"cache_read_input_tokens":39951,"cache_creation":{"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":7739,"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":0},"output_tokens":34,"service_tier":"standard","inference_geo":"not_available"},"context_management":null},"parent_tool_use_id":"toolu_01VRb8XuH96FMHgWe6LjJa4W","session_id":"157c1bc9-ab32-4587-b8cf-8c8afacd8f33","uuid":"8fd377c8-19f1-4d3c-a396-8a3e0a44e8fe"}
{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_01TXxCmWqnbTTxWcBhCFraYf","type":"tool_result","content":"     1→import { NextRequest, NextResponse } from \"next/server\";\n     2→import db from \"@/lib/db\";\n     3→import {\n     4→  createTextChannel,\n     5→  createChannelInvite,\n     6→  sendChannelMessage,\n     7→  isValidSnowflake,\n     8→} from \"@/lib/discord\";\n     9→import { createNotification } from \"@/lib/notifications\";\n    10→import { safeCompare } from \"@/lib/crypto-utils\";\n    11→\n    12→/**\n    13→ * Discord webhook endpoint for bot events.\n    14→ *\n    15→ * This endpoint handles:\n    16→ * 1. POST with { type: \"link\", guildId, listingId } - Link a Discord server to a listing\n    17→ *    (called by the bot or by an admin setup flow)\n    18→ *\n    19→ * Note: Unlike Telegram's push-based webhook, Discord uses Gateway (WebSocket)\n    20→ * for real-time events. This endpoint is used as an HTTP callback for\n    21→ * simpler integration scenarios or manual triggering.\n    22→ */\n    23→export async function POST(req: NextRequest) {\n    24→  // Verify webhook secret — fail closed if not configured\n    25→  const webhookSecret = process.env.DISCORD_WEBHOOK_SECRET;\n    26→  if (!webhookSecret) {\n    27→    return NextResponse.json({ error: \"Webhook not configured\" }, { status: 500 });\n    28→  }\n    29→  const headerSecret = req.headers.get(\"X-Discord-Webhook-Secret\");\n    30→  if (!headerSecret || !safeCompare(headerSecret, webhookSecret)) {\n    31→    return NextResponse.json({ error: \"Unauthorized\" }, { status: 403 });\n    32→  }\n    33→\n    34→  try {\n    35→    const body = await req.json();\n    36→    const { type } = body;\n    37→\n    38→    if (type === \"link\") {\n    39→      return handleLink(body);\n    40→    }\n    41→\n    42→    // Discord Interaction verification (for future slash command support)\n    43→    if (type === 1) {\n    44→      // PING - respond with PONG\n    45→      return NextResponse.json({ type: 1 });\n    46→    }\n    47→\n    48→    return NextResponse.json({ error: \"Unknown event type\" }, { status: 400 });\n    49→  } catch (err) {\n    50→    console.error(\"Discord webhook error:\", err);\n    51→    return NextResponse.json(\n    52→      { error: \"Internal server error\" },\n    53→      { status: 500 }\n    54→    );\n    55→  }\n    56→}\n    57→\n    58→async function handleLink(body: {\n    59→  guildId: string;\n    60→  listingId: number;\n    61→  channelId?: string;\n    62→}) {\n    63→  const { guildId, listingId, channelId } = body;\n    64→\n    65→  if (!guildId || !listingId) {\n    66→    return NextResponse.json(\n    67→      { error: \"Missing guildId or listingId\" },\n    68→      { status: 400 }\n    69→    );\n    70→  }\n    71→\n    72→  if (typeof listingId !== \"number\" || !Number.isInteger(listingId) || listingId < 1) {\n    73→    return NextResponse.json(\n    74→      { error: \"Invalid listingId\" },\n    75→      { status: 400 }\n    76→    );\n    77→  }\n    78→\n    79→  // Validate Discord snowflake IDs to prevent path traversal in API URLs\n    80→  if (typeof guildId !== \"string\" || !isValidSnowflake(guildId)) {\n    81→    return NextResponse.json(\n    82→      { error: \"Invalid guildId format\" },\n    83→      { status: 400 }\n    84→    );\n    85→  }\n    86→  if (channelId !== undefined && (typeof channelId !== \"string\" || !isValidSnowflake(channelId))) {\n    87→    return NextResponse.json(\n    88→      { error: \"Invalid channelId format\" },\n    89→      { status: 400 }\n    90→    );\n    91→  }\n    92→\n    93→  const listing = db\n    94→    .prepare(\"SELECT * FROM listings WHERE id = ?\")\n    95→    .get(listingId) as Record<string, unknown> | undefined;\n    96→\n    97→  if (!listing) {\n    98→    return NextResponse.json({ error: \"Listing not found\" }, { status: 404 });\n    99→  }\n   100→\n   101→  if (!listing.is_full) {\n   102→    return NextResponse.json(\n   103→      { error: \"Cannot link Discord until the group is full\" },\n   104→      { status: 400 }\n   105→    );\n   106→  }\n   107→\n   108→  if (listing.discord_link) {\n   109→    return NextResponse.json(\n   110→      { error: \"Discord link already set for this listing\" },\n   111→      { status: 400 }\n   112→    );\n   113→  }\n   114→\n   115→  let targetChannelId = channelId;\n   116→\n   117→  // If no channel specified, create one\n   118→  if (!targetChannelId) {\n   119→    const bookTitle = String(listing.book_title || \"reading-group\");\n   120→    const channelName = `bookmate-${bookTitle\n   121→      .toLowerCase()\n   122→      .replace(/[^a-z0-9]+/g, \"-\")\n   123→      .slice(0, 80)}`;\n   124→\n   125→    const channel = await createTextChannel(\n   126→      guildId,\n   127→      channelName,\n   128→      `Bookmate reading group for \"${listing.book_title}\" by ${listing.book_author}`\n   129→    );\n   130→\n   131→    if (!channel) {\n   132→      return NextResponse.json(\n   133→        { error: \"Failed to create Discord channel. Make sure the bot has Manage Channels permission.\" },\n   134→        { status: 500 }\n   135→      );\n   136→    }\n   137→\n   138→    targetChannelId = channel.id;\n   139→  }\n   140→\n   141→  // Create invite link\n   142→  const inviteLink = await createChannelInvite(targetChannelId);\n   143→  if (!inviteLink) {\n   144→    return NextResponse.json(\n   145→      { error: \"Failed to create invite link. Make sure the bot has Create Instant Invite permission.\" },\n   146→      { status: 500 }\n   147→    );\n   148→  }\n   149→\n   150→  // Save to DB\n   151→  db.prepare(\n   152→    \"UPDATE listings SET discord_link = ?, discord_channel_id = ? WHERE id = ?\"\n   153→  ).run(inviteLink, targetChannelId, listingId);\n   154→\n   155→  // Notify all members\n   156→  const members = db\n   157→    .prepare(\"SELECT user_id FROM listing_members WHERE listing_id = ?\")\n   158→    .all(listingId) as { user_id: number }[];\n   159→\n   160→  for (const member of members) {\n   161→    createNotification(\n   162→      member.user_id,\n   163→      listingId,\n   164→      \"discord_ready\",\n   165→      `The Discord channel for \"${listing.book_title}\" is ready! Join to start discussing.`\n   166→    );\n   167→  }\n   168→\n   169→  // Send welcome message to the channel\n   170→  await sendChannelMessage(\n   171→    targetChannelId,\n   172→    `Welcome to the **${listing.book_title}** reading group! 📚\\n\\n` +\n   173→      `This channel was created by Bookmate for your reading group.\\n` +\n   174→      `**Reading pace:** ${listing.reading_pace}\\n` +\n   175→      `**Start date:** ${listing.start_date}\\n\\n` +\n   176→      `Happy reading! 🎉`\n   177→  );\n   178→\n   179→  return NextResponse.json({ ok: true, inviteLink });\n   180→}\n   181→"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":"toolu_01VRb8XuH96FMHgWe6LjJa4W","session_id":"157c1bc9-ab32-4587-b8cf-8c8afacd8f33","uuid":"7523bb78-db67-4114-8f1b-af5f4da76933","timestamp":"2026-03-24T10:01:55.261Z"}

{"type":"assistant","message":{"model":"claude-sonnet-4-6","id":"msg_01SPGDmAS3P2N6ZrRXFwCDcL","type":"message","role":"assistant","content":[{"type":"tool_use","id":"toolu_01Uuf3BfaxysMCULzezy4rW8","name":"Read","input":{"file_path":"/root/Projects/bookmate/app/lib/discord.ts"},"caller":{"type":"direct"}}],"stop_reason":null,"stop_sequence":null,"usage":{"input_tokens":1,"cache_creation_input_tokens":7739,"cache_read_input_tokens":39951,"cache_creation":{"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":7739,"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":0},"output_tokens":34,"service_tier":"standard","inference_geo":"not_available"},"context_management":null},"parent_tool_use_id":"toolu_01VRb8XuH96FMHgWe6LjJa4W","session_id":"157c1bc9-ab32-4587-b8cf-8c8afacd8f33","uuid":"155c866d-d0fb-41cb-8116-64d745c32319"}
{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_01Uuf3BfaxysMCULzezy4rW8","type":"tool_result","content":"     1→/**\n     2→ * Discord Bot API integration for automatic server/channel creation.\n     3→ *\n     4→ * When DISCORD_BOT_TOKEN and DISCORD_CLIENT_ID are set, the system can:\n     5→ * 1. Generate bot invite URLs so authors can add the bot to their server\n     6→ * 2. Create text channels and invite links via Discord REST API\n     7→ * 3. Receive interaction events via webhook\n     8→ *\n     9→ * Flow:\n    10→ * - Author clicks \"Set up Discord\" → opens bot invite URL\n    11→ * - Author adds bot to their server\n    12→ * - Bot receives an interaction or the author triggers channel creation\n    13→ * - Bot creates a text channel + invite link, saves to listing\n    14→ */\n    15→\n    16→const BOT_TOKEN = process.env.DISCORD_BOT_TOKEN || \"\";\n    17→const CLIENT_ID = process.env.DISCORD_CLIENT_ID || \"\";\n    18→const DISCORD_API = \"https://discord.com/api/v10\";\n    19→\n    20→export function isBotConfigured(): boolean {\n    21→  return BOT_TOKEN.length > 0 && CLIENT_ID.length > 0;\n    22→}\n    23→\n    24→/**\n    25→ * Validate a Discord snowflake ID (numeric string, 17-20 digits).\n    26→ * Used to prevent path traversal in Discord API URL construction.\n    27→ */\n    28→export function isValidSnowflake(id: string): boolean {\n    29→  return /^\\d{17,20}$/.test(id);\n    30→}\n    31→\n    32→/**\n    33→ * Call the Discord REST API.\n    34→ */\n    35→async function callApi<T = unknown>(\n    36→  endpoint: string,\n    37→  method: string = \"GET\",\n    38→  body?: Record<string, unknown>\n    39→): Promise<{ ok: boolean; data?: T; error?: string }> {\n    40→  try {\n    41→    const res = await fetch(`${DISCORD_API}${endpoint}`, {\n    42→      method,\n    43→      headers: {\n    44→        Authorization: `Bot ${BOT_TOKEN}`,\n    45→        \"Content-Type\": \"application/json\",\n    46→      },\n    47→      body: body ? JSON.stringify(body) : undefined,\n    48→    });\n    49→\n    50→    if (!res.ok) {\n    51→      const errData = await res.json().catch(() => ({}));\n    52→      return {\n    53→        ok: false,\n    54→        error: (errData as Record<string, string>).message || `HTTP ${res.status}`,\n    55→      };\n    56→    }\n    57→\n    58→    const data = (await res.json()) as T;\n    59→    return { ok: true, data };\n    60→  } catch (err) {\n    61→    return { ok: false, error: String(err) };\n    62→  }\n    63→}\n    64→\n    65→/**\n    66→ * Get bot user info.\n    67→ */\n    68→export async function getBotInfo(): Promise<{\n    69→  id: string;\n    70→  username: string;\n    71→} | null> {\n    72→  const res = await callApi<{ id: string; username: string }>(\"/users/@me\");\n    73→  return res.ok && res.data ? res.data : null;\n    74→}\n    75→\n    76→/**\n    77→ * Generate a bot invite URL that includes the listing ID in the state parameter.\n    78→ * The bot needs permissions: Manage Channels, Create Instant Invite, Send Messages\n    79→ *\n    80→ * Permission bits:\n    81→ * - MANAGE_CHANNELS (0x10) = 16\n    82→ * - CREATE_INSTANT_INVITE (0x1) = 1\n    83→ * - SEND_MESSAGES (0x800) = 2048\n    84→ * - VIEW_CHANNEL (0x400) = 1024\n    85→ * Total: 3089\n    86→ */\n    87→export function generateBotInviteUrl(listingId: number): string {\n    88→  const permissions = 3089;\n    89→  const state = `listing_${listingId}`;\n    90→  return `https://discord.com/oauth2/authorize?client_id=${CLIENT_ID}&permissions=${permissions}&scope=bot&state=${encodeURIComponent(state)}`;\n    91→}\n    92→\n    93→/**\n    94→ * Create a text channel in a guild.\n    95→ */\n    96→export async function createTextChannel(\n    97→  guildId: string,\n    98→  name: string,\n    99→  topic?: string\n   100→): Promise<{ id: string; name: string } | null> {\n   101→  const res = await callApi<{ id: string; name: string }>(\n   102→    `/guilds/${guildId}/channels`,\n   103→    \"POST\",\n   104→    {\n   105→      name: name.toLowerCase().replace(/[^a-z0-9-]/g, \"-\").slice(0, 100),\n   106→      type: 0, // GUILD_TEXT\n   107→      topic: topic || \"Bookmate reading group discussion channel\",\n   108→    }\n   109→  );\n   110→  return res.ok && res.data ? res.data : null;\n   111→}\n   112→\n   113→/**\n   114→ * Create an invite for a channel.\n   115→ */\n   116→export async function createChannelInvite(\n   117→  channelId: string,\n   118→  maxAge: number = 0 // 0 = never expires\n   119→): Promise<string | null> {\n   120→  const res = await callApi<{ code: string }>(\n   121→    `/channels/${channelId}/invites`,\n   122→    \"POST\",\n   123→    {\n   124→      max_age: maxAge,\n   125→      max_uses: 0,\n   126→      unique: true,\n   127→    }\n   128→  );\n   129→  if (res.ok && res.data) {\n   130→    return `https://discord.gg/${res.data.code}`;\n   131→  }\n   132→  return null;\n   133→}\n   134→\n   135→/**\n   136→ * Send a message to a Discord channel.\n   137→ */\n   138→export async function sendChannelMessage(\n   139→  channelId: string,\n   140→  content: string\n   141→): Promise<boolean> {\n   142→  const res = await callApi(\n   143→    `/channels/${channelId}/messages`,\n   144→    \"POST\",\n   145→    { content }\n   146→  );\n   147→  return res.ok;\n   148→}\n   149→\n   150→/**\n   151→ * Get guild (server) info.\n   152→ */\n   153→export async function getGuild(\n   154→  guildId: string\n   155→): Promise<{ id: string; name: string } | null> {\n   156→  const res = await callApi<{ id: string; name: string }>(\n   157→    `/guilds/${guildId}`\n   158→  );\n   159→  return res.ok && res.data ? res.data : null;\n   160→}\n   161→\n   162→/**\n   163→ * List guilds the bot is a member of.\n   164→ */\n   165→export async function getBotGuilds(): Promise<\n   166→  { id: string; name: string }[]\n   167→> {\n   168→  const res = await callApi<{ id: string; name: string }[]>(\n   169→    \"/users/@me/guilds\"\n   170→  );\n   171→  return res.ok && res.data ? res.data : [];\n   172→}\n   173→\n   174→/**\n   175→ * Parse listing ID from a state or payload string.\n   176→ * Expected format: \"listing_123\"\n   177→ */\n   178→export function parseListingIdFromState(state: string): number | null {\n   179→  const match = state.match(/^listing_(\\d+)$/);\n   180→  return match ? parseInt(match[1], 10) : null;\n   181→}\n   182→"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":"toolu_01VRb8XuH96FMHgWe6LjJa4W","session_id":"157c1bc9-ab32-4587-b8cf-8c8afacd8f33","uuid":"bdb108a3-822b-4e47-b6e1-905c44acd2dc","timestamp":"2026-03-24T10:01:55.541Z"}
{"type":"system","subtype":"task_progress","task_id":"abacbd2cccabbeabe","tool_use_id":"toolu_01VRb8XuH96FMHgWe6LjJa4W","description":"Reading app/api/notifications/route.ts","usage":{"total_tokens":58019,"tool_uses":31,"duration_ms":48112},"last_tool_name":"Read","uuid":"8de8117f-4017-42a1-928f-56f0badb992f","session_id":"157c1bc9-ab32-4587-b8cf-8c8afacd8f33"}
{"type":"assistant","message":{"model":"claude-sonnet-4-6","id":"msg_01NCd7xMUqhyZUiFGxWu3LTW","type":"message","role":"assistant","content":[{"type":"tool_use","id":"toolu_01JwzdQ7pQTv8wKFj1UbuuoY","name":"Read","input":{"file_path":"/root/Projects/bookmate/app/app/api/notifications/route.ts"},"caller":{"type":"direct"}}],"stop_reason":null,"stop_sequence":null,"usage":{"input_tokens":1,"cache_creation_input_tokens":9273,"cache_read_input_tokens":47690,"cache_creation":{"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":9273,"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":0},"output_tokens":34,"service_tier":"standard","inference_geo":"not_available"},"context_management":null},"parent_tool_use_id":"toolu_01VRb8XuH96FMHgWe6LjJa4W","session_id":"157c1bc9-ab32-4587-b8cf-8c8afacd8f33","uuid":"d161a134-352b-490f-b634-c09b617b0830"}
{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_01JwzdQ7pQTv8wKFj1UbuuoY","type":"tool_result","content":"     1→import { NextRequest, NextResponse } from \"next/server\";\n     2→import db from \"@/lib/db\";\n     3→import { requireAuth } from \"@/lib/session\";\n     4→\n     5→export async function GET() {\n     6→  const session = await requireAuth();\n     7→  if (!session) {\n     8→    return NextResponse.json({ error: \"Authentication required\" }, { status: 401 });\n     9→  }\n    10→\n    11→  const notifications = db\n    12→    .prepare(\n    13→      `SELECT n.*, l.book_title\n    14→       FROM notifications n\n    15→       LEFT JOIN listings l ON n.listing_id = l.id\n    16→       WHERE n.user_id = ?\n    17→       ORDER BY n.created_at DESC\n    18→       LIMIT 50`\n    19→    )\n    20→    .all(session.userId);\n    21→\n    22→  const unreadCount = db\n    23→    .prepare(\n    24→      \"SELECT COUNT(*) as count FROM notifications WHERE user_id = ? AND is_read = 0\"\n    25→    )\n    26→    .get(session.userId) as { count: number };\n    27→\n    28→  return NextResponse.json({\n    29→    notifications,\n    30→    unreadCount: unreadCount.count,\n    31→  });\n    32→}\n    33→\n    34→export async function PATCH(req: NextRequest) {\n    35→  const session = await requireAuth();\n    36→  if (!session) {\n    37→    return NextResponse.json({ error: \"Authentication required\" }, { status: 401 });\n    38→  }\n    39→\n    40→  const { notificationId } = await req.json();\n    41→\n    42→  if (notificationId) {\n    43→    // Validate notificationId is a positive integer\n    44→    if (typeof notificationId !== \"number\" || !Number.isInteger(notificationId) || notificationId < 1) {\n    45→      return NextResponse.json({ error: \"Invalid notification ID\" }, { status: 400 });\n    46→    }\n    47→    db.prepare(\n    48→      \"UPDATE notifications SET is_read = 1 WHERE id = ? AND user_id = ?\"\n    49→    ).run(notificationId, session.userId);\n    50→  } else {\n    51→    // Mark all as read\n    52→    db.prepare(\n    53→      \"UPDATE notifications SET is_read = 1 WHERE user_id = ?\"\n    54→    ).run(session.userId);\n    55→  }\n    56→\n    57→  return NextResponse.json({ ok: true });\n    58→}\n    59→"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":"toolu_01VRb8XuH96FMHgWe6LjJa4W","session_id":"157c1bc9-ab32-4587-b8cf-8c8afacd8f33","uuid":"cdae3ef8-1f01-4437-930b-732bdb6fa3a5","timestamp":"2026-03-24T10:01:59.272Z"}

{"type":"assistant","message":{"model":"claude-sonnet-4-6","id":"msg_01NCd7xMUqhyZUiFGxWu3LTW","type":"message","role":"assistant","content":[{"type":"tool_use","id":"toolu_01UnnrW2psa63FSWB4dUzpWF","name":"Read","input":{"file_path":"/root/Projects/bookmate/app/app/api/profile/reputation/route.ts"},"caller":{"type":"direct"}}],"stop_reason":null,"stop_sequence":null,"usage":{"input_tokens":1,"cache_creation_input_tokens":9273,"cache_read_input_tokens":47690,"cache_creation":{"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":9273,"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":0},"output_tokens":34,"service_tier":"standard","inference_geo":"not_available"},"context_management":null},"parent_tool_use_id":"toolu_01VRb8XuH96FMHgWe6LjJa4W","session_id":"157c1bc9-ab32-4587-b8cf-8c8afacd8f33","uuid":"68ee5a7b-38f8-40c7-88d1-d34f3acee0d3"}
{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_01UnnrW2psa63FSWB4dUzpWF","type":"tool_result","content":"     1→import { NextRequest, NextResponse } from \"next/server\";\n     2→import db from \"@/lib/db\";\n     3→import { requireAuth } from \"@/lib/session\";\n     4→\n     5→/**\n     6→ * GET /api/profile/reputation?userId=X\n     7→ *\n     8→ * Returns a user's reputation score and rating breakdown.\n     9→ * If no userId is provided, returns the current user's reputation.\n    10→ */\n    11→export async function GET(req: NextRequest) {\n    12→  const session = await requireAuth();\n    13→  if (!session) {\n    14→    return NextResponse.json(\n    15→      { error: \"Authentication required\" },\n    16→      { status: 401 }\n    17→    );\n    18→  }\n    19→\n    20→  const userIdParam = req.nextUrl.searchParams.get(\"userId\");\n    21→  const userId = userIdParam ? parseInt(userIdParam, 10) : session.userId;\n    22→  const isOwnProfile = userId === session.userId;\n    23→\n    24→  if (userIdParam && isNaN(userId as number)) {\n    25→    return NextResponse.json(\n    26→      { error: \"Invalid userId parameter\" },\n    27→      { status: 400 }\n    28→    );\n    29→  }\n    30→\n    31→  // Average score\n    32→  const stats = db\n    33→    .prepare(\n    34→      `SELECT\n    35→        COUNT(*) as total_ratings,\n    36→        ROUND(AVG(score), 1) as average_score,\n    37→        SUM(CASE WHEN score = 5 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) as five_star,\n    38→        SUM(CASE WHEN score = 4 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) as four_star,\n    39→        SUM(CASE WHEN score = 3 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) as three_star,\n    40→        SUM(CASE WHEN score = 2 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) as two_star,\n    41→        SUM(CASE WHEN score = 1 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) as one_star\n    42→       FROM ratings\n    43→       WHERE rated_user_id = ?`\n    44→    )\n    45→    .get(userId) as {\n    46→      total_ratings: number;\n    47→      average_score: number | null;\n    48→      five_star: number;\n    49→      four_star: number;\n    50→      three_star: number;\n    51→      two_star: number;\n    52→      one_star: number;\n    53→    };\n    54→\n    55→  // Number of completed groups\n    56→  const completedGroups = db\n    57→    .prepare(\n    58→      `SELECT COUNT(*) as count\n    59→       FROM listing_members lm\n    60→       JOIN listings l ON lm.listing_id = l.id\n    61→       WHERE lm.user_id = ? AND l.is_full = 1`\n    62→    )\n    63→    .get(userId) as { count: number };\n    64→\n    65→  // Number of groups where this user has given ratings\n    66→  const groupsRated = db\n    67→    .prepare(\n    68→      `SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT listing_id) as count\n    69→       FROM ratings\n    70→       WHERE rater_id = ?`\n    71→    )\n    72→    .get(userId) as { count: number };\n    73→\n    74→  // Recent ratings received (with anonymized feedback)\n    75→  const recentRatings = db\n    76→    .prepare(\n    77→      `SELECT r.score, r.comment, r.created_at, l.book_title\n    78→       FROM ratings r\n    79→       JOIN listings l ON r.listing_id = l.id\n    80→       WHERE r.rated_user_id = ?\n    81→       ORDER BY r.created_at DESC\n    82→       LIMIT 10`\n    83→    )\n    84→    .all(userId) as {\n    85→      score: number;\n    86→      comment: string;\n    87→      created_at: string;\n    88→      book_title: string;\n    89→    }[];\n    90→\n    91→  // Strip comments from ratings when viewing other users' profiles (privacy)\n    92→  const sanitizedRatings = isOwnProfile\n    93→    ? recentRatings\n    94→    : recentRatings.map(({ score, created_at, book_title }) => ({\n    95→        score,\n    96→        created_at,\n    97→        book_title,\n    98→      }));\n    99→\n   100→  return NextResponse.json({\n   101→    reputation: {\n   102→      averageScore: stats.average_score || 0,\n   103→      totalRatings: stats.total_ratings,\n   104→      completedGroups: completedGroups.count,\n   105→      groupsRated: groupsRated.count,\n   106→      breakdown: {\n   107→        5: stats.five_star || 0,\n   108→        4: stats.four_star || 0,\n   109→        3: stats.three_star || 0,\n   110→        2: stats.two_star || 0,\n   111→        1: stats.one_star || 0,\n   112→      },\n   113→      recentRatings: sanitizedRatings,\n   114→    },\n   115→  });\n   116→}\n   117→"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":"toolu_01VRb8XuH96FMHgWe6LjJa4W","session_id":"157c1bc9-ab32-4587-b8cf-8c8afacd8f33","uuid":"baf464ca-020c-44ab-b66c-a26fb2a2ffbd","timestamp":"2026-03-24T10:01:59.664Z"}

{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_01LV7e6fJQPFmjPpxCVnZ77M","type":"tool_result","content":"     1→import nodemailer from \"nodemailer\";\n     2→\n     3→const isConfigured =\n     4→  !!process.env.SMTP_HOST &&\n     5→  !!process.env.SMTP_PORT &&\n     6→  !!process.env.SMTP_FROM;\n     7→\n     8→let transporter: nodemailer.Transporter | null = null;\n     9→\n    10→function getTransporter(): nodemailer.Transporter | null {\n    11→  if (!isConfigured) return null;\n    12→  if (!transporter) {\n    13→    transporter = nodemailer.createTransport({\n    14→      host: process.env.SMTP_HOST,\n    15→      port: parseInt(process.env.SMTP_PORT || \"587\", 10),\n    16→      secure: process.env.SMTP_SECURE === \"true\",\n    17→      ...(process.env.SMTP_USER && process.env.SMTP_PASS\n    18→        ? {\n    19→            auth: {\n    20→              user: process.env.SMTP_USER,\n    21→              pass: process.env.SMTP_PASS,\n    22→            },\n    23→          }\n    24→        : {}),\n    25→    });\n    26→  }\n    27→  return transporter;\n    28→}\n    29→\n    30→export async function sendEmail(\n    31→  to: string,\n    32→  subject: string,\n    33→  text: string,\n    34→  html?: string\n    35→): Promise<boolean> {\n    36→  const t = getTransporter();\n    37→  if (!t) return false;\n    38→\n    39→  try {\n    40→    await t.sendMail({\n    41→      from: process.env.SMTP_FROM,\n    42→      to,\n    43→      subject,\n    44→      text,\n    45→      html: html || text,\n    46→    });\n    47→    return true;\n    48→  } catch (err) {\n    49→    console.error(\"Failed to send email:\", err);\n    50→    return false;\n    51→  }\n    52→}\n    53→\n    54→export function isEmailConfigured(): boolean {\n    55→  return isConfigured;\n    56→}\n    57→"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":"toolu_01VRb8XuH96FMHgWe6LjJa4W","session_id":"157c1bc9-ab32-4587-b8cf-8c8afacd8f33","uuid":"7b489854-8f87-46de-8f1d-11e4d63e28c1","timestamp":"2026-03-24T10:02:00.211Z"}

{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_01KvvDWmQctVvazZxvZ31aoU","type":"tool_result","content":"     1→import { NextRequest, NextResponse } from \"next/server\";\n     2→import { isBotConfigured, setWebhook, getBotInfo } from \"@/lib/telegram\";\n     3→import { safeCompare } from \"@/lib/crypto-utils\";\n     4→\n     5→/**\n     6→ * POST /api/telegram/setup\n     7→ *\n     8→ * Sets up the Telegram webhook. Should be called once after deployment.\n     9→ * Requires TELEGRAM_BOT_TOKEN and TELEGRAM_WEBHOOK_URL env vars.\n    10→ *\n    11→ * Protected by a setup secret to prevent unauthorized access.\n    12→ */\n    13→export async function POST(req: NextRequest) {\n    14→  const setupSecret = process.env.TELEGRAM_SETUP_SECRET;\n    15→  if (!setupSecret) {\n    16→    return NextResponse.json(\n    17→      { error: \"TELEGRAM_SETUP_SECRET not configured\" },\n    18→      { status: 500 }\n    19→    );\n    20→  }\n    21→\n    22→  const body = await req.json();\n    23→  if (!body.secret || typeof body.secret !== \"string\" || !safeCompare(body.secret, setupSecret)) {\n    24→    return NextResponse.json({ error: \"Unauthorized\" }, { status: 403 });\n    25→  }\n    26→\n    27→  if (!isBotConfigured()) {\n    28→    return NextResponse.json(\n    29→      { error: \"TELEGRAM_BOT_TOKEN not configured\" },\n    30→      { status: 500 }\n    31→    );\n    32→  }\n    33→\n    34→  const webhookUrl = process.env.TELEGRAM_WEBHOOK_URL;\n    35→  if (!webhookUrl) {\n    36→    return NextResponse.json(\n    37→      { error: \"TELEGRAM_WEBHOOK_URL not configured\" },\n    38→      { status: 500 }\n    39→    );\n    40→  }\n    41→\n    42→  const bot = await getBotInfo();\n    43→  if (!bot) {\n    44→    return NextResponse.json(\n    45→      { error: \"Failed to connect to Telegram API. Check your bot token.\" },\n    46→      { status: 500 }\n    47→    );\n    48→  }\n    49→\n    50→  const success = await setWebhook(webhookUrl);\n    51→  if (!success) {\n    52→    return NextResponse.json(\n    53→      { error: \"Failed to set webhook\" },\n    54→      { status: 500 }\n    55→    );\n    56→  }\n    57→\n    58→  return NextResponse.json({\n    59→    ok: true,\n    60→    bot: { username: bot.username, id: bot.id },\n    61→    webhookUrl,\n    62→  });\n    63→}\n    64→\n    65→/**\n    66→ * GET /api/telegram/setup\n    67→ *\n    68→ * Returns the current Telegram bot configuration status.\n    69→ */\n    70→export async function GET() {\n    71→  return NextResponse.json({\n    72→    botConfigured: isBotConfigured(),\n    73→    webhookUrlConfigured: !!process.env.TELEGRAM_WEBHOOK_URL,\n    74→  });\n    75→}\n    76→"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":"toolu_01VRb8XuH96FMHgWe6LjJa4W","session_id":"157c1bc9-ab32-4587-b8cf-8c8afacd8f33","uuid":"aa7c01e4-0bd7-4d34-8000-6d1b363916fe","timestamp":"2026-03-24T10:02:05.204Z"}

{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_01FyJMKWrQLkWygdYQcL8R1K","type":"tool_result","content":"     1→import { timingSafeEqual } from \"crypto\";\n     2→\n     3→/**\n     4→ * Constant-time string comparison to prevent timing side-channel attacks.\n     5→ * Use this for comparing secrets (webhook tokens, setup secrets, etc.)\n     6→ */\n     7→export function safeCompare(a: string, b: string): boolean {\n     8→  const bufA = Buffer.from(a);\n     9→  const bufB = Buffer.from(b);\n    10→  if (bufA.length !== bufB.length) {\n    11→    // Do a dummy comparison to add some constant work, though this does not\n    12→    // fully mask length differences (bufA self-compare time varies with length).\n    13→    // In practice, the secrets compared here are fixed-length env vars.\n    14→    timingSafeEqual(bufA, bufA);\n    15→    return false;\n    16→  }\n    17→  return timingSafeEqual(bufA, bufB);\n    18→}\n    19→\n    20→/**\n    21→ * Escape user-controlled content for safe embedding in HTML emails.\n    22→ */\n    23→export function escapeHtmlForEmail(text: string): string {\n    24→  return text\n    25→    .replace(/&/g, \"&amp;\")\n    26→    .replace(/</g, \"&lt;\")\n    27→    .replace(/>/g, \"&gt;\")\n    28→    .replace(/\"/g, \"&quot;\")\n    29→    .replace(/'/g, \"&#039;\");\n    30→}\n    31→"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":"toolu_01VRb8XuH96FMHgWe6LjJa4W","session_id":"157c1bc9-ab32-4587-b8cf-8c8afacd8f33","uuid":"ea80823f-b446-4460-b72a-a51760c5d044","timestamp":"2026-03-24T10:02:11.329Z"}

{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_01BFd7RQ1emvAxouXnGX1TMk","type":"tool_result","content":"     1→\"use client\";\n     2→\n     3→import { useEffect, useState, useCallback, useRef } from \"react\";\n     4→import { useRouter } from \"next/navigation\";\n     5→import Link from \"next/link\";\n     6→import BookCover from \"@/components/BookCover\";\n     7→\n     8→interface ReadingItem {\n     9→  id: number;\n    10→  book_title: string;\n    11→  book_author: string;\n    12→  book_cover_url: string;\n    13→  reading_pace: string;\n    14→  start_date: string;\n    15→  meeting_format: string;\n    16→  is_full: number;\n    17→  telegram_link: string;\n    18→  member_count: number;\n    19→  max_group_size: number;\n    20→  author_name: string;\n    21→  joined_at: string;\n    22→}\n    23→\n    24→interface Reputation {\n    25→  averageScore: number;\n    26→  totalRatings: number;\n    27→  completedGroups: number;\n    28→  groupsRated: number;\n    29→  breakdown: Record<number, number>;\n    30→  recentRatings: {\n    31→    score: number;\n    32→    comment: string;\n    33→    created_at: string;\n    34→    book_title: string;\n    35→  }[];\n    36→}\n    37→\n    38→type TabKey = \"profile\" | \"reading\" | \"genres\" | \"reputation\";\n    39→\n    40→export default function ProfilePage() {\n    41→  const router = useRouter();\n    42→  const [displayName, setDisplayName] = useState(\"\");\n    43→  const [bio, setBio] = useState(\"\");\n    44→  const [email, setEmail] = useState(\"\");\n    45→  const [loading, setLoading] = useState(true);\n    46→  const [saving, setSaving] = useState(false);\n    47→  const [message, setMessage] = useState(\"\");\n    48→\n    49→  // Tab state\n    50→  const [activeTab, setActiveTab] = useState<TabKey>(\"profile\");\n    51→\n    52→  // Reading activity\n    53→  const [active, setActive] = useState<ReadingItem[]>([]);\n    54→  const [history, setHistory] = useState<ReadingItem[]>([]);\n    55→  const [upcoming, setUpcoming] = useState<ReadingItem[]>([]);\n    56→  const [readingLoading, setReadingLoading] = useState(false);\n    57→\n    58→  // Genres\n    59→  const [userGenres, setUserGenres] = useState<string[]>([]);\n    60→  const [availableGenres, setAvailableGenres] = useState<string[]>([]);\n    61→  const [genresLoading, setGenresLoading] = useState(false);\n    62→  const [genresSaving, setGenresSaving] = useState(false);\n    63→  const [genresMessage, setGenresMessage] = useState(\"\");\n    64→\n    65→  // Reputation\n    66→  const [reputation, setReputation] = useState<Reputation | null>(null);\n    67→  const [reputationLoading, setReputationLoading] = useState(false);\n    68→\n    69→  // Track which tabs have been fetched to avoid redundant requests\n    70→  const fetchedTabs = useRef<Set<string>>(new Set());\n    71→\n    72→  useEffect(() => {\n    73→    fetch(\"/api/auth/me\")\n    74→      .then((r) => r.json())\n    75→      .then((data) => {\n    76→        if (!data.user) {\n    77→          router.push(\"/auth/login\");\n    78→          return;\n    79→        }\n    80→        setDisplayName(data.user.displayName);\n    81→        setBio(data.user.bio || \"\");\n    82→        setEmail(data.user.email);\n    83→        setLoading(false);\n    84→      })\n    85→      .catch(() => {\n    86→        // Network error — can't verify auth, redirect to login\n    87→        router.push(\"/auth/login\");\n    88→      });\n    89→  }, [router]);\n    90→\n    91→  const fetchReading = useCallback(() => {\n    92→    setReadingLoading(true);\n    93→    fetch(\"/api/profile/reading\")\n    94→      .then((r) => r.json())\n    95→      .then((data) => {\n    96→        setActive(data.active || []);\n    97→        setHistory(data.history || []);\n    98→        setUpcoming(data.upcoming || []);\n    99→        setReadingLoading(false);\n   100→      })"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":"toolu_01VRb8XuH96FMHgWe6LjJa4W","session_id":"157c1bc9-ab32-4587-b8cf-8c8afacd8f33","uuid":"318a5700-53e1-4fb5-bf26-4d337b669d60","timestamp":"2026-03-24T10:02:15.789Z"}

1 : 0);\n   245→    }\n   246→    if (platformPreference !== undefined) {\n   247→      updates.push(\"platform_preference = ?\");\n   248→      values.push(platformPreference);\n   249→    }\n   250→\n   251→    if (updates.length === 0) {\n   252→      return NextResponse.json({ error: \"No fields to update\" }, { status: 400 });\n   253→    }\n   254→\n   255→    // Wrap in transaction to prevent race between is_full recalculation and concurrent joins\n   256→    const updateTransaction = db.transaction(() => {\n   257→      if (needsIsFullRecalc) {\n   258→        // Re-read member count and is_full inside transaction to prevent stale data\n   259→        const fresh = db.prepare(\"SELECT is_full FROM listings WHERE id = ?\").get(listingId) as { is_full: number } | undefined;\n   260→        const currentMemberCount = (db\n   261→          .prepare(\"SELECT COUNT(*) as count FROM listing_members WHERE listing_id = ?\")\n   262→          .get(listingId) as { count: number }).count;\n   263→\n   264→        if (parsedNewSize < currentMemberCount) {\n   265→          return { error: `Cannot reduce group size below current member count (${currentMemberCount})` };\n   266→        }\n   267→\n   268→        if (parsedNewSize > currentMemberCount && fresh?.is_full) {\n   269→          updates.push(\"is_full = ?\");\n   270→          values.push(0);\n   271→        }\n   272→      }\n   273→\n   274→      values.push(listingId);\n   275→      db.prepare(`UPDATE listings SET ${updates.join(\", \")} WHERE id = ?`).run(...values);\n   276→      return null;\n   277→    });"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":"toolu_01VRb8XuH96FMHgWe6LjJa4W","session_id":"157c1bc9-ab32-4587-b8cf-8c8afacd8f33","uuid":"1397f834-63d8-49c0-9881-3ccd1d7c13a7","timestamp":"2026-03-24T10:02:42.022Z"}

{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_01NhpEFLioaKXUHVacXAdBpk","type":"tool_result","content":"50-\n51-  // PRD: full listings should be invisible to non-participants\n52:  if (listing.is_full && !isMember && !isAuthor) {\n53-    return NextResponse.json(\n54-      { error: \"This listing is no longer available\" },\n--\n231-      values.push(meetingFormat);\n232-    }\n233:    // Track whether maxGroupSize change needs is_full recalculation\n234-    let needsIsFullRecalc = false;\n235-    let parsedNewSize = 0;\n--\n253-    }\n254-\n255:    // Wrap in transaction to prevent race between is_full recalculation and concurrent joins\n256-    const updateTransaction = db.transaction(() => {\n257-      if (needsIsFullRecalc) {\n258:        // Re-read member count and is_full inside transaction to prevent stale data\n259:        const fresh = db.prepare(\"SELECT is_full FROM listings WHERE id = ?\").get(listingId) as { is_full: number } | undefined;\n260-        const currentMemberCount = (db\n261-          .prepare(\"SELECT COUNT(*) as count FROM listing_members WHERE listing_id = ?\")\n--\n266-        }\n267-\n268:        if (parsedNewSize > currentMemberCount && fresh?.is_full) {\n269:          updates.push(\"is_full = ?\");\n270-          values.push(0);\n271-        }"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":"toolu_01VRb8XuH96FMHgWe6LjJa4W","session_id":"157c1bc9-ab32-4587-b8cf-8c8afacd8f33","uuid":"9c391800-5a5a-43e1-8615-ad1f181d9723","timestamp":"2026-03-24T10:03:00.088Z"}

{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_01VRb8XuH96FMHgWe6LjJa4W","type":"tool_result","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Now I have a complete picture. Let me compile all findings.\n\n---\n\n## Code Review — Bookmate (Round 13)\n\nReviewing `/root/Projects/bookmate/app` with focus on the six specified areas.\n\n---\n\n## CRITICAL Issues\n\n### 1. PATCH /api/listings/[id] — Transaction Closure Mutates Outer Arrays, Producing a Data Corruption Bug on Retry or Re-invocation\n\n**File:** `/root/Projects/bookmate/app/app/api/listings/[id]/route.ts`\n**Lines:** 214–215, 256–276\n**Confidence: 90**\n\nThe `updates` and `values` arrays are declared outside the `db.transaction()` closure (lines 214–215) and then mutated inside it (lines 269–270, 274). This is a latent data-corruption bug with two real consequences:\n\n**Consequence A — `better-sqlite3` serializable transaction retry.** When two concurrent PATCH requests hit the same listing, SQLite will serialize them. The second call to `updateTransaction()` on a retry path would push additional elements onto the same `updates` and `values` arrays that were already mutated by the first attempt, producing a SQL statement like:\n\n```sql\nUPDATE listings SET max_group_size = ?, is_full = ?, is_full = ? WHERE id = ?, ?\n```\n\nThis would either crash with a SQLite error or produce a silent double-bind that updates `is_full` to the wrong value.\n\n**Consequence B — The `is_full = 0` reset is only appended inside the transaction.** If `needsIsFullRecalc` is true and the fresh read determines the group is full but the new size is larger, `\"is_full = ?\"` is pushed to `updates` inside the transaction function. This means on the very first call path it works correctly, but it's structurally fragile — the transaction logic should not close over and mutate outer mutable state. If the transaction function is ever called more than once (e.g., via a developer adding retry logic), it will corrupt the SQL.\n\n**Fix:** Snapshot `updates` and `values` inside the transaction function, or construct the entire SQL string inside it:\n\n```typescript\nconst updateTransaction = db.transaction(() => {\n  const txUpdates = [...updates];\n  const txValues = [...values];\n\n  if (needsIsFullRecalc) {\n    const fresh = ...;\n    const currentMemberCount = ...;\n    if (parsedNewSize < currentMemberCount) {\n      return { error: `Cannot reduce group size below current member count (${currentMemberCount})` };\n    }\n    if (parsedNewSize > currentMemberCount && fresh?.is_full) {\n      txUpdates.push(\"is_full = ?\");\n      txValues.push(0);\n    }\n  }\n\n  txValues.push(listingId);\n  db.prepare(`UPDATE listings SET ${txUpdates.join(\", \")} WHERE id = ?`).run(...txValues);\n  return null;\n});\n```\n\n---\n\n### 2. PATCH /api/listings/[id] — `is_full` Is Never Set to 1 When `maxGroupSize` Is Reduced to Exactly the Current Member Count\n\n**File:** `/root/Projects/bookmate/app/app/api/listings/[id]/route.ts`\n**Lines:** 264–271\n**Confidence: 95**\n\nThe transaction checks `parsedNewSize < currentMemberCount` (correctly rejects) and `parsedNewSize > currentMemberCount && fresh?.is_full` (correctly un-flags full). But there is no branch for `parsedNewSize === currentMemberCount`. If the author reduces `max_group_size` from 10 to 5 when there are currently 5 members, `is_full` should be set to `1` (group is now full). This case is silently ignored: the group stays with `is_full = 0`, continues appearing in the public listing browse, and the join endpoint will continue accepting new members — until the join transaction's own count check fires.\n\nIn practice this means: after a PATCH that sets `max_group_size` exactly equal to current member count, the listing remains visible and joinable in the browse view (`l.is_full = 0` filter on `/api/listings`), allowing a race-window join that the join transaction will then reject internally. The UX is broken (users see a joinable listing that immediately returns an error), and the `is_full` flag is stale.\n\n**Fix:** Add an `else if` branch:\n\n```typescript\nif (parsedNewSize < currentMemberCount) {\n  return { error: `Cannot reduce group size below current member count (${currentMemberCount})` };\n} else if (parsedNewSize === currentMemberCount && !fresh?.is_full) {\n  txUpdates.push(\"is_full = ?\");\n  txValues.push(1);\n} else if (parsedNewSize > currentMemberCount && fresh?.is_full) {\n  txUpdates.push(\"is_full = ?\");\n  txValues.push(0);\n}\n```\n\n---\n\n## IMPORTANT Issues\n\n### 3. `notifyGroupFull` Sends Group-Full Notification to the Author, Who Is Counted as a Member — But the Author Did Not Join via the Join Endpoint\n\n**File:** `/root/Projects/bookmate/app/lib/notifications.ts`\n**Lines:** 121–148\n**Confidence: 85**\n\n`notifyGroupFull` fetches all rows from `listing_members` for the listing (line 127–128). The author is auto-added to `listing_members` at creation time (see `create/route.ts` line 134–136). So when the group fills, the author receives a \"group is full\" notification telling them \"the bot will set up the chat automatically.\"\n\nThis is intentional per the comment on line 125: `\"author needs to know group is full to create the chat\"`. However, the join endpoint at `/api/listings/[id]/join/route.ts` lines 110–114 has special logic that skips the `new_member` notification for the author when the group fills, specifically to avoid sending a redundant notification. But `notifyGroupFull` also fires separately (line 111), which sends a second notification to the author for the same event.\n\nThe result is: when the last member joins, the author gets **one** `group_full` notification from `notifyGroupFull` (which includes the author in the members query). This is the intended behavior, but the comment is misleading and the join/notify logic is easy to break. The real problem is that in the approval flow (`applications/route.ts` lines 121–124), `notifyGroupFull` is called AND then `notifyApplicationDecision` for all rejected applicants is called. Meanwhile `notifyGroupFull` already sends to all `listing_members` (including the author). This is correct, but the `group_full` message text says \"The bot will set up automatically\" regardless of whether the author has already set up the Telegram/Discord link manually.\n\n**Specific bug:** In `notifyGroupFull` (line 135–137), the bot-configured check reads from `process.env` at runtime. This is fine. But the message says \"The organizer will share a link soon\" when the bot is not configured — even though for an approval-flow group that just became full, the author might already have a link. Since no link check against the DB is performed here, members receive misleading messaging. This is a UX correctness issue rather than a data integrity bug.\n\n**Confidence drops to 82 — this is a real UX issue but not a data corruption risk.**\n\n---\n\n### 4. `POST /api/listings/[id]/apply` — Re-application After Rejection Does Not Notify the Author\n\n**File:** `/root/Projects/bookmate/app/app/api/listings/[id]/apply/route.ts`\n**Lines:** 81–86, 103–104\n**Confidence: 85**\n\nWhen a previously-rejected user re-applies (the `existingApp.status === 'rejected'` branch at line 81), the code updates the status back to `'pending'` and returns `{ error: null, status: 200 }`. Execution then falls through to line 103–104 which calls `notifyApplicationReceived`. This part works correctly.\n\nHowever, the `message` field in the application is never updated on re-application. More critically: the `applied_at` timestamp is reset inside the transaction (`applied_at = datetime('now')`), but the original application `id` is retained. This means the organizer's applicant list will show the correct `applied_at` date. This is fine.\n\nThe actual bug is that the `pendingApplicants` query in the GET endpoint (`route.ts` line 78–86 in `/api/listings/[id]/route.ts`) orders by `la.applied_at ASC`. A re-applicant's `applied_at` is updated to now, so they'll correctly appear at the end of the queue. This is correct behavior.\n\n**Re-evaluating: this is not a bug.** Dropping this finding.\n\n---\n\n### 5. `/api/listings/[id]/route.ts` GET — `pendingApplicants` Returned to Author Even When `requires_approval` Is False (After Toggle)\n\n**File:** `/root/Projects/bookmate/app/app/api/listings/[id]/route.ts`\n**Lines:** 77, 79–86\n**Confidence: 88**\n\nThe `pendingApplicants` query at line 77 is only executed `if (isAuthor && listing.requires_approval)`. This correctly gates the expensive query. However, the returned JSON always includes `pendingApplicants` (line 108), and it's initialized to `[]` when not fetched (line 76). This means that if `requires_approval` was `true`, the author approved some applicants, then toggled `requires_approval` back to `false`, any remaining `pending` applications in the DB will never be surfaced to the author in the UI, even though those applicants are in a limbo state (pending status, no way to be decided).\n\nThis is a data consistency issue: `listing_applications` rows can exist with `status = 'pending'` for a listing where `requires_approval = 0`. Those users cannot join directly (the `/join` endpoint blocks them at line 57–68 for having a pending application), and the author never sees them to approve/reject them. The applicant is stuck.\n\n**The join endpoint check at lines 56–68 of `join/route.ts` exacerbates this:** even though `requires_approval` is now `false`, any user with an existing pending application cannot join directly (correct for preventing double-join), but they also cannot re-apply (the `apply` endpoint blocks at line 36: `if (!listing.requires_approval)`), and their application is invisible to the author. They are permanently locked out unless they contact the author out-of-band or the author re-enables `requires_approval`.\n\n**Fix:** When `requires_approval` is toggled from `1` to `0`, auto-reject all pending applications in the same PATCH transaction, or surface pending applications to the author regardless of the current `requires_approval` flag.\n\n---\n\n### 6. Session Data Is Stale After Profile Update — `displayName` in Session Not Validated Against DB Length Limits\n\n**File:** `/root/Projects/bookmate/app/app/api/profile/route.ts`\n**Lines:** 32–33\n**Confidence: 80**\n\nWhen a user updates their profile, the session is updated in-place at line 32–33. The `displayName` in the session is then used as the display name in notification messages (e.g., `join/route.ts` line 113: `session.displayName || \"Someone\"`). This is correct for the current request, but since iron-session cookies are encrypted client-side, a user with an **active old session** will continue to see their old `displayName` in the Navbar until they re-authenticate (iron-session has no server-side revocation).\n\nThis is an expected iron-session limitation and not a bug per se. However, the `requireAuth` function at `session.ts` line 37–43 returns the session as-is without re-validating the user exists in the DB. If a user's account is deleted from the DB while they have an active session, `requireAuth` returns a valid session object and all authenticated endpoints proceed as if the user exists. Subsequent DB queries that require the user to exist will return undefined, but most endpoints don't handle that — they just proceed (e.g., creating a notification for a deleted user would insert a `user_id` that no longer exists in the `users` table, violating the foreign key `FOREIGN KEY (user_id) REFERENCES users(id)` with `PRAGMA foreign_keys = ON`).\n\n**Concrete failure path:** Admin manually deletes a user from the DB. That user's session cookie is still valid. They POST to `/api/listings/create`, which inserts a listing with their deleted `author_id`. SQLite with `foreign_keys = ON` will reject this with a constraint error, which is caught by the catch block and returns a 500. The real issue is the 500 rather than a meaningful \"account not found\" 401. This is a minor operational concern for this app since there's no admin deletion UI, but it's architecturally correct to flag.\n\n**Confidence: 80 — real but low practical impact for this app's current scope.**\n\n---\n\n## Summary\n\n| # | Severity | File | Lines | Issue |\n|---|----------|------|-------|-------|\n| 1 | CRITICAL | `/app/api/listings/[id]/route.ts` | 214–275 | PATCH transaction closes over and mutates outer `updates`/`values` arrays — structurally unsafe, breaks on any retry |\n| 2 | CRITICAL | `/app/api/listings/[id]/route.ts` | 264–271 | `is_full` never set to `1` when `maxGroupSize` is reduced to exactly equal current member count |\n| 3 | IMPORTANT | `/app/api/listings/[id]/route.ts` + `apply/route.ts` | 77, 57–68 | Users with pending applications become permanently locked out when `requires_approval` is toggled off — invisible to author and blocked from direct join |\n| 4 | IMPORTANT | `/lib/session.ts` | 37–43 | `requireAuth` does not re-validate user existence in DB; deleted users with live sessions cause FK-violation 500s instead of 401s |\n\nIssues 1 and 2 are the most actionable. Issue 3 requires a schema/flow decision. Issue 4 is low-priority for this app's current operational model."},{"type":"text","text":"agentId: abacbd2cccabbeabe (use SendMessage with to: 'abacbd2cccabbeabe' to continue this agent)\n<usage>total_tokens: 84001\ntool_uses: 50\nduration_ms: 183173</usage>"}]}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"157c1bc9-ab32-4587-b8cf-8c8afacd8f33","uuid":"f9027c36-e207-40a1-bdc4-65a9aae9cd8c","timestamp":"2026-03-24T10:04:14.322Z","tool_use_result":{"status":"completed","prompt":"Review the Bookmate codebase at /root/Projects/bookmate/app for bugs, logic errors, security vulnerabilities, and code quality issues. \n\nThis is a Next.js 15 app with SQLite (better-sqlite3), iron-session auth, and Tailwind CSS. The app lets users create reading group listings, join them, and connect via Telegram/Discord.\n\nIMPORTANT: 12 rounds of code review have already been completed. Focus on areas that are less commonly reviewed:\n\n1. **Database schema integrity** — check for missing indexes, constraint gaps, migration issues\n2. **API route edge cases** — unusual input combinations, boundary conditions, empty states  \n3. **Session/auth edge cases** — session expiry, concurrent sessions, cookie handling\n4. **Client-side state management** — stale closures, race conditions in effects, memory leaks\n5. **Error recovery paths** — what happens when external services fail (Open Library API down, email sending fails)\n6. **Data consistency** — are there code paths where the DB could end up in an inconsistent state?\n\nKey files to review:\n- app/lib/db.ts (schema and queries)\n- app/app/api/ (all API routes)\n- app/components/ (React components)\n- app/lib/session.ts, app/lib/notifications.ts\n- app/app/listings/[id]/page.tsx (complex client component)\n\nSkip issues that are trivial or cosmetic. Only report HIGH confidence findings that could cause real problems. For each finding, specify the file, line numbers, severity (CRITICAL/IMPORTANT/MINOR), and a clear explanation of the bug.","agentId":"abacbd2cccabbeabe","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Now I have a complete picture. The second call to `updateTransaction()` on a retry path would push additional elements onto the same `updates` and `values` arrays that were already mutated by the first attempt, producing a SQL statement like:\n\n```sql\nUPDATE listings SET max_group_size = ?, is_full = ?, is_full = ? WHERE id = ?, ?\n```\n\nThis would either crash with a SQLite error or produce a silent double-bind that updates `is_full` to the wrong value.\n\n**Consequence B — The `is_full = 0` reset is only appended inside the transaction.** If `needsIsFullRecalc` is true and the fresh read determines the group is full but the new size is larger, `\"is_full = ?\"` is pushed to `updates` inside the transaction function. This case is silently ignored: the group stays with `is_full = 0`, continues appearing in the public listing browse, and the join endpoint will continue accepting new members — until the join transaction's own count check fires.\n\nIn practice this means: after a PATCH that sets `max_group_size` exactly equal to current member count, the listing remains visible and joinable in the browse view (`l.is_full = 0` filter on `/api/listings`), allowing a race-window join that the join transaction will then reject internally. The UX is broken (users see a joinable listing that immediately returns an error), and the `is_full` flag is stale.\n\n**Fix:** Add an `else if` branch:\n\n```typescript\nif (parsedNewSize < currentMemberCount) {\n  return { error: `Cannot reduce group size below current member count (${currentMemberCount})` };\n} else if (parsedNewSize === currentMemberCount && !fresh?.is_full) {\n  txUpdates.push(\"is_full = ?\");\n  txValues.push(1);\n} else if (parsedNewSize > currentMemberCount && fresh?.is_full) {\n  txUpdates.push(\"is_full = ?\");\n  txValues.push(0);\n}\n```\n\n---\n\n## IMPORTANT Issues\n\n### 3. `notifyGroupFull` Sends Group-Full Notification to the Author, Who Is Counted as a Member — But the Author Did Not Join via the Join Endpoint\n\n**File:** `/root/Projects/bookmate/app/lib/notifications.ts`\n**Lines:** 121–148\n**Confidence: 85**\n\n`notifyGroupFull` fetches all rows from `listing_members` for the listing (line 127–128).
